install.description: Choose an install path, review readiness, and start setup without leaving the desktop app.

uninstall.description: Review the detected {{product}} runtime and remove the current installation with the matching uninstall path.

migrate.description: Choose sources to import, review detected folders, and migrate {{product}} content into Claw Studio.

assessment.title: Environment check

installation.description: See how this profile installs the product, which directories it manages, and which alternatives remain documented only.
installation.noAlternatives: No additional documented install paths were provided for this profile.
installation.noDirectories: This profile does not declare managed directory metadata.
installation.notAvailable: This profile does not expose structured installation metadata.

dataLayout.description: Review what data this product creates, what should be backed up, and what remains after uninstall.
dataLayout.empty: This profile does not declare structured data layout metadata.

migration.description: Review what can be migrated, which commands are available, and what still requires manual review.
migration.empty: This profile does not declare migration strategies.

assessment.blockedDescription: Resolve the blocking issues above before starting installation.

openclaw.name: OpenClaw
openclaw.description: Personal AI assistant with onboarding, channels, skills, and dashboard.

zeroclaw.name: ZeroClaw
zeroclaw.description: Ultra-light Rust-native runtime for edge and low-resource deployments.

ironclaw.name: IronClaw
ironclaw.description: Security-first Rust-native assistant with database-backed memory and routines.

openclaw.recommendedReason: OpenClaw docs recommend onboarding-first setup, and Windows hosts are usually most stable through WSL2.
openclaw.firstRun: Finish provider setup, choose channels, and add starter skills before your first live workflow.

prerequisites.one: Node 24 is recommended. Node 22.16+ is still supported.
prerequisites.two: Windows should prefer WSL2 for the cleanest day-to-day runtime.
prerequisites.three: Docker is optional for isolated gateways, not the default install path.

zeroclaw.recommendedPath: Clone the repo and install with Cargo.
zeroclaw.recommendedReason: The install manifests and vendor docs both use the Rust source profile; the old pnpm alias is only a compatibility shim.
zeroclaw.firstRun: Confirm `zeroclaw --version` works, then continue with your own provider, channel, and hardware tuning.

prerequisites.one: Install Git plus the Rust toolchain with Cargo.
prerequisites.two: Windows builds should have Visual Studio Build Tools or an equivalent C++ toolchain.
prerequisites.three: ZeroClaw is optimized for tiny memory budgets and low-cost hardware.

ironclaw.recommendedPath: Clone the repo, build with Cargo, then run onboarding.
ironclaw.recommendedReason: IronClaw is source-first today and its README explicitly requires PostgreSQL + pgvector plus NEAR AI authentication.
ironclaw.firstRun: Create the database, enable pgvector, then run `ironclaw onboard` to finish auth and secret setup.

prerequisites.one: Install Git, Rust, and Cargo before building.
prerequisites.two: Prepare PostgreSQL + pgvector for memory and search features.
prerequisites.three: Keep NEAR AI credentials ready for the setup wizard.

wsl.title: WSL install
wsl.description: Install OpenClaw inside WSL on Windows for a Linux-style runtime path.

docker.title: Docker install
docker.description: Install with Docker and support local Docker or Docker running inside WSL.

npm.title: npm install
npm.description: Install globally with npm when Node.js is already available on the host.

pnpm.title: pnpm install
pnpm.description: Install globally with pnpm for teams already using a pnpm-based toolchain.

source.title: Source install
source.description: Clone, build, and run the selected product from source.

zeroclawSource.title: Rust source install
zeroclawSource.description: Build ZeroClaw from source with Git + Cargo for the official Rust-native workflow.

ironclawSource.title: Secure source install
ironclawSource.description: Build IronClaw from source with Rust, then prepare PostgreSQL + pgvector and onboarding.

cloud.title: Cloud install
cloud.description: Provision the selected product in the cloud. This mode is in development.

install.sectionHeading: Ready to install on this device
install.sectionDescription: Pick a product, compare the paths that fit this machine, and start with the option that gets you running fastest.

autoDetection.title: Automatic detection
autoDetection.heading: We checked this machine for the fastest safe install path
autoDetection.description: The recommendation below combines operating system, architecture, runtime readiness, and dependency checks so you can start with the most suitable path.

recommendationReasons.platformPreferred: {{method}} is the best fit for the platform and runtime we detected on this device.
recommendationReasons.bestReadyFallback: {{method}} is the fastest install path that is ready to run on this machine right now.
recommendationReasons.requiresSetup: {{method}} still looks like the best long-term fit, but this machine needs prerequisite fixes first.
recommendationReasons.installed: {{method}} already appears to be installed on this machine, so you can continue with setup and verification.
recommendationReasons.singlePath: This product currently exposes one supported install path, so we are using it as the default recommendation.

method.title: Choose install method
method.description: Review the selected path and why it is recommended on this device.

prepare.title: Prepare environment
prepare.description: Check dependencies, blockers, and auto-fix options before installation.

install.title: Run installation
install.description: Start the real installer and follow the live execution output.

complete.title: Complete installation
complete.description: Review the result, paths, and the next action.

prepare.heading: Prepare environment
prepare.description: Make sure this method is ready, then continue to the real install.
prepare.dependenciesDescription: Use auto-fix when the installer can remediate a missing dependency.

install.heading: Run installation
install.description: This launches the real installer flow for the selected method.

complete.successDescription: {{method}} finished and the runtime paths are ready to review.
complete.errorDescription: {{method}} did not finish cleanly. Review the output and retry when ready.

systemRequirements.title: System requirements
systemRequirements.description: A modern CPU, stable network access, and enough disk space for {{product}} artifacts are recommended for the smoothest setup.

uninstall.title: Uninstall {{product}}
uninstall.description: Pick the uninstall mode that matches the current {{product}} installation and remove the existing runtime cleanly.
uninstall.reviewHeading: Confirm the runtime before you remove it
uninstall.reviewDescription: Review the current {{product}} runtime before removing it.
uninstall.noInstallDetectedDescription: No existing {{product}} installation was found on this system. Switch to the Install tab to set one up.

wsl.description: Remove the OpenClaw runtime that was installed inside WSL.

docker.description: Stop and remove the OpenClaw Docker deployment.

npm.description: Remove the global OpenClaw package that was installed through npm.

pnpm.description: Remove the global OpenClaw package that was installed through pnpm.

source.description: Remove the source-linked OpenClaw runtime and local build output.

zeroclawSource.description: Remove the Cargo-installed ZeroClaw binary and local source checkout while keeping workspace data available for migration.

ironclawSource.description: Remove the Cargo-installed IronClaw binary and source checkout. PostgreSQL-backed data stays in your database until you export or drop it separately.

migrate.title: Migrate {{product}}
migrate.description: Import settings, workspace data, and related files from {{product}} into Claw Studio.
migrate.productDescription: Import settings, workspace data, and related files from {{product}} into Claw Studio.
migrate.readyDescription: Review the detected folders, choose what should be imported, and start the migration when you are ready.

config.title: Configuration
config.description: Import configuration files and local preferences.

data.title: Data
data.description: Import local data files and runtime state.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.description: Import the working directory and related project content.

logs.title: Logs
logs.description: Import diagnostic logs that still need to be kept.

manual.title: Manual folder
manual.description: Import a custom folder when the automatic scan misses the original location.

ironclaw.title: Database export still required
ironclaw.description: IronClaw stores its primary memory, routines, and chat data in PostgreSQL. PostgreSQL-backed data is not copied automatically by this file importer, so export the database separately before uninstalling the old runtime.

output.title: Migration output
output.missing: The source folder could not be found.
